stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Yaa Gyasi is a Ghanaian-born American author of novels.
  • She published the novel Homegoing in 2016.
  • A central character in the novel is a young woman living in eighteenth-century Ghana.
  • Another central character is her half-sister, sold into slavery and taken to the American South.
stem: The student wants to introduce the novel Homegoing to an audience already familiar with Yaa Gyasi. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Yaa Gyasi's novel Homegoing centers around two half-sisters, one living in eighteenth-century Ghana and the other sold into slavery and taken to the American South.
Yaa Gyasi's settings include both eighteenth-century Ghana and the American South.
In addition to her novel Homegoing, which was published in 2016, Ghanaian-born author Yaa Gyasi has written other novels.
Homegoing was not the only novel written by Ghanaian-born author Yaa Gyasi.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence effectively introduces Homegoing to an audience already familiar with Gyasi, explaining that the novel centers on two half-sisters and mentioning the author without providing redundant background information.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence provides a detail about Gyasi's settings; it does not introduce, or even mention, the novel. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence provides introductory information about Gyasi; it does not effectively introduce the novel to an audience already familiar with her. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence provides introductory information about Gyasi; it does not effectively introduce the novel to an audience already familiar with her.
